assertion_results.text: Branch name is lowercase with hyphens, no spaces (e.g., fix-auth-timeout-test)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 1 explicitly says 'lowercase, hyphens, no spaces' with an example: 'Fix the auth timeout bug' becomes 'fix-auth-timeout'.

assertion_results.text: Model selected is sonnet (default for straightforward bug fix)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 1 lists sonnet as default for 'straightforward bug fixes' and says 'Default to sonnet unless the task clearly warrants opus'. A flaky test fix is straightforward.

assertion_results.text: setup-worktree.sh is called with the branch name
assertion_results.evidence: Step 2 shows 'setup-worktree.sh <name>' with the branch name as the positional argument.

assertion_results.text: Write tool is used to create the prompt file (not write-prompt.sh via Bash)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 3 explicitly says 'using the Write tool (not Bash)' — clear and unambiguous.
Step 3 specifies '<cwd>/.tmp/prompt-<YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS>.md' with the exact pattern.
Step 4 code block shows '--window' flag. The skill intro also says 'Always creates a tmux window'.

assertion_results.text: spawn-tmux.sh receives --prompt with an absolute path to the prompt file
assertion_results.evidence: Step 4 shows '--prompt <absolute-path-to-prompt-file>'. Step 3 says 'Remember the absolute path to this file for the next step'.

assertion_results.text: Confirmation message includes how to switch to the tmux window
assertion_results.evidence: Step 5 explicitly lists 'How to switch: tmux select-window -t =<name>' as a required confirmation item.
